plan_adherence_notes: All 9 plan steps were implemented exactly as specified: test file created with makeBucket() helper, 7 test cases covering SVG rendering, bar count, height proportionality, trade count labels, empty state, x-axis time labels, and y-axis currency labels. All tests pass (7/7). Decisions d1-d5 from the plan were all followed — tooltip interpreted as trade count labels, empty state tests current behavior, proportionality via ratio comparison, plain render() used, and makeBucket() factory created.
missed_questions: How should different interval prop values ('hour', 'day', 'week') affect test coverage? Only 'hour' was tested.
Should the labelEvery skip logic be explicitly tested (e.g., with >5 buckets to verify only every Nth label renders)?
Should edge cases like a single bucket be tested separately, since the component has special xCenter logic for length===1?
reason: Execution faithfully followed the plan with all 9 steps completed. Research was thorough — correctly identified that tooltip means trade count labels, validated all 5 assumptions, and recommended resilient testing approaches. Tests are well-structured: they use the existing project patterns (Vitest + RTL + container.querySelectorAll for SVG), the makeBucket factory reduces boilerplate, and the proportionality test uses ratio comparison with toBeCloseTo rather than brittle pixel values. All 7 tests pass green. Minor gaps in question coverage (interval variants, labelEvery skip logic, single-bucket edge case) are reasonable omissions that don't undermine the core test suite.
